homepage.text_excerpt: Max Wilkinson MP Skip to main content Max Wilkinson MP Contact Max Meet Max Wilkinson Max's Campaigns News Donate Join Donate Join Max's Campaigns Film-style age limits for social media Max on Race Week Max on the Environment Max on the Economy Max on the NHS New GP Surgery for Cheltenham Back Jools' Law Reopen The Aveta Birth Centre Don't Let Cheltenham Lose Its Voice Save Our Recycling Centre 2025 Back Max's Sunshine Bill Scrap Labour's Winter Fuel Cuts Fix Our High Street News 2026 2025 2024 Max Wilkinson MP Max is Cheltenham's MP - Fighting for a fair deal for our town Back Max's Campaign Max is supporting bereaved parents seeking answers from social media giants Back Jools' Law Stand up to social media giants Sign the petition View Meet Max Wilkinson Max is Cheltenham's man in Westminster. Elected as our MP in July 2024, he is already hitting the ground running, campaigning for our town. Living in Fairview with his wife and daughter, Max has helped Cheltenham make progress on issues ranging from affordable housing to the environment and the cost of living over a decade as a Councillor. How can Max help? Find out how Max Wilkinson MP can help you Get in touch Find out how to contact Max Wilkinson MP Contact Max View Working for us Find out what Max is campaigning on Max's Campaigns View Max in Parliament View Max's voting record and work in Parliament Find out more View UK Lib Dem news Lib Dems Winning Getting the job done: Our latest wins in Westminster The Liberal Democrats secured a number of wins in Parliament recently - here's a recap of what we've achieved. 2 May 2026 View Guaranteeing GP surgeries on new developments Liberal Democrats are clear: new housing must go hand in hand with new GPs. 14 Apr 2026 View Cost of living Our emergency plan to keep Britain moving Liberal Democrats are calling for an emergency transport package to keep Britain moving, cutting the cost of travel for people – whether by car, train or bus. h2: Potholes, fly-tipping or issues in your area?
h3: Report missed bin collections, abandoned vehicles, fly-tipping, graffiti, dog fouling and other issues in your area with Fix My Street.
h2: Need advice on an issue?
h3: You can find written advice guides on a range of issues, from debt to housing, from Citizens Advice.
h2: Need support from the Council?
h3: Cheltenham Borough Council covers many local issues, from bin collections to climate change.

pages.title: Film-style age limits for social media - Max Wilkinson MP
pages.h1: Film-style age limits for social media
pages.text_excerpt: Film-style age limits for social media - Max Wilkinson MP Skip to main content Max Wilkinson MP Contact Max Meet Max Wilkinson Max's Campaigns News Donate Join Donate Join Max's Campaigns Film-style age limits for social media Max on Race Week Max on the Environment Max on the Economy Max on the NHS New GP Surgery for Cheltenham Back Jools' Law Reopen The Aveta Birth Centre Don't Let Cheltenham Lose Its Voice Save Our Recycling Centre 2025 Back Max's Sunshine Bill Scrap Labour's Winter Fuel Cuts Fix Our High Street News 2026 2025 2024 Film-style age limits for social media Max is calling for the default minimum age for access to social media to be raised to 16. Parents are rightly concerned about the dangerous content their children are being exposed to online. Max is now calling for film-style age ratings of up to 18 for the most harmful platforms, and a default rating of 16 for social media. This means that access to social media platforms would be restricted based on the risk they pose to young people. Social media channels like TikTok, Snapchat, Instagram and Facebook would all be legally classified as unsuitable for under-16s. If a platform spreads harmful content or relies on addictive and harmful algorithms, it should not be allowed anywhere near our children. That means platforms that continue to host extreme content, like X, could be rated 18+. Since becoming an MP, Max has supported Cheltenham resident Ellen Roome in her campaign to hold social media companies to account for their failure to protect children online. Last year, Max introduced the Jools' Law private member's bill to Parliament, which would give parents access to their deceased children’s social media accounts to allow proper investigations in the case of the death of a child. Introduce film-style age limits for social media We the undersigned Cheltenham residents, back calls for default minimum age for social media access to be set at 16. Oops! pages.title: New GP Surgery for Cheltenham - Max Wilkinson MP
headings: h2: Cheltenham Needs A New GP Surgery
h3: Back Max's campaign for the local healthcare we deserve
pages.text_excerpt: New GP Surgery for Cheltenham - Max Wilkinson MP Skip to main content Max Wilkinson MP Contact Max Meet Max Wilkinson Max's Campaigns News Donate Join Donate Join Max's Campaigns Film-style age limits for social media Max on Race Week Max on the Environment Max on the Economy Max on the NHS New GP Surgery for Cheltenham Back Jools' Law Reopen The Aveta Birth Centre Don't Let Cheltenham Lose Its Voice Save Our Recycling Centre 2025 Back Max's Sunshine Bill Scrap Labour's Winter Fuel Cuts Fix Our High Street News 2026 2025 2024 Cheltenham Needs A New GP Surgery Back Max's campaign for the local healthcare we deserve Cheltenham’s Conservative MP previously announced plans to bring back 24/7 doctor-led A&E at Cheltenham General and improve GP services. As I speak to residents across Cheltenham, facing ever-longer waits for appointments and basic services, they're asking me what went wrong. Demand for appointments is increasing because of failures elsewhere in the healthcare system. Local surgeries have warned that GPs are working long hours without breaks. That's why I'm launching this campaign: local people deserve better than these Tory failures on the health service. I'm in discussions with our local health service and have called on local councils to put this project on their infrastructure funding list. Will you sign my petition to help show local health bosses that Cheltenham needs a new GP surgery? Back Max's Campaign for a new GP surgery A new GP surgery is needed in Cheltenham to help people get quicker appointments to see their doctor. Back my campaign to secure the funding we need for the health service we deserve. Oops! Please check the fields below and submit again. Looks like there was a problem with the submission.
